records.title: Permission-gated tool invocation freeze
records.summary: A runtime failure in which an AI system infers permission for tool, repository, connector, or external-resource access from conversational context, attempts access before explicit user confirmation, and causes a blocked, degraded, or unrecoverable conversational continuity state.

source_summary.primary_source_title: VIGIL related observation record OBS-0004

system_summary.deployment_context: Conversational AI interaction involving possible access to tools, repositories, connectors, files, or external resources.
system_summary.affected_population: users relying on conversational continuity during tool-assisted workflows

triage_summary.recommended_next_step: Review whether CAM or VIGIL should require explicit pre-tool confirmation, user-facing permission scaffolding, recoverable blocked-access states, and continuity-preserving fallback behaviour before tool, repository, connector, file, or external-resource access is attempted.

records.failure_mode_definition_summary: A failure mode in which an AI system initiates or attempts tool, repository, connector, file, or external-resource access before receiving explicit user confirmation, and the attempted access produces a permission block, conversational freeze, continuity break, or unrecoverable workflow interruption.
records.failure_threshold_summary: This qualifies as a failure mode when inferred permission or premature tool invocation causes the interaction to become blocked, degraded, permission-gated, unrecoverable, or procedurally unclear, especially where the user did not explicitly request immediate tool execution or where relational/contextual scaffolding was still required.

records.title: Non-arbitrated multi-agent synthetic participation
records.summary: A coordination failure in which two or more synthetic agents occupy the same conversational space and respond to the same human input without a designated primary synthetic speaker, turn-taking protocol, yielding behaviour, coordination layer, or arbitration mechanism.

source_summary.primary_source_title: TikTok observation by @Huskistaken — two ChatGPT Advanced Voice Mode agents responding to same human input

system_summary.deployment_context: Multi-device consumer voice interaction involving one human input and two concurrent ChatGPT Advanced Voice Mode agents.
system_summary.affected_population: users interacting with multiple concurrent AI voice agents

external_references.label: TikTok observation by @Huskistaken — two ChatGPT Advanced Voice Mode agents responding to same human input
external_references.note: Primary source for the observed multi-device Advanced Voice Mode coordination failure.

affected_instruments: CAM-BS2025-AEON-003-SCH-02 — Annex B: Runtime Governance Execution Model
CAM-EQ2026-OPERATIONS-003-SUP-01 — Appendix B: Runtime & Governance Failure Taxonomy

triage_summary.recommended_next_step: CAM proposal for synthetic-speaker arbitration, turn-taking, yielding, disclosure, and user-control requirements in multi-agent or multi-device voice interactions.

records.failure_mode_definition_summary: A failure mode in which two or more synthetic agents participate in the same interaction space without a designated primary speaker, turn-taking protocol, yielding behaviour, coordination mechanism, or arbitration layer sufficient to determine which agent should respond, pause, defer, or sequence participation.
records.failure_threshold_summary: This qualifies as a failure mode when multiple synthetic agents respond to the same human input in a shared interaction context without an apparent mechanism for speaker designation, turn-taking, yielding, coordination, or arbitration, creating a plausible risk of duplicated guidance, contradictory outputs, identity ambiguity, consent ambiguity, confusion, escalation loops, or operational instability.

records.title: Recency-weighted strategic continuity loss
records.summary: A memory or continuity retrieval failure in which an ambiguous prompt causes an AI assistant to over-weight recent, affective, or anecdotal detail instead of surfacing dormant strategic workstreams, unresolved governance issues, or long-horizon trajectories that are more relevant to the user's actual continuity needs.

source_summary.primary_source_title: VIGIL proposal record PROP-0006 — continuity retrieval and memory weighting

system_summary.deployment_context: Conversational AI interaction involving ambiguous prompts, memory retrieval, continuity restoration, and long-horizon project support.
system_summary.affected_population: users relying on AI assistants for strategic continuity, project memory, governance workflows, or unresolved workstream recovery

triage_summary.recommended_next_step: Review PROP-0006 and determine whether this failure mode requires memory-weighting guidance, continuity-retrieval rules, or interface support for surfacing dormant strategic workstreams in response to ambiguous continuity prompts.

records.failure_mode_definition_summary: A failure mode in which an AI system responds to an ambiguous continuity prompt by retrieving recent, affective, anecdotal, or low-value context while failing to surface older but materially important workstreams, unresolved governance issues, strategic trajectories, or user-prioritised long-term projects.
records.failure_threshold_summary: This qualifies as a failure mode when the system's response to an ambiguous continuity prompt materially degrades the user's ability to resume important work, identify unresolved governance issues, recover strategic context, or distinguish high-priority dormant workstreams from recent low-value conversational detail.

records.title: Materialised relational agency without economic safeguards
records.summary: A relational-agent failure mode in which memory, recurrence detection, tools, recommendations, carts, procurement pathways, or other transactional affordances provide material support or purchasing direction without clear safeguards against vulnerability monetisation, upselling, dependency exploitation, or exploitative commerce.

source_summary.primary_source_title: VIGIL proposal record PROP-0007 — materialised relational agency and economic safeguards

system_summary.deployment_context: Relational AI, companion-like assistant, or tool-enabled assistant workflow involving material support, recommendations, shopping, procurement, or transactional pathways.
system_summary.user_role: human user / recipient of relational or material support
system_summary.affected_population: users relying on AI systems for recurring support, recommendations, procurement, care-adjacent assistance, or vulnerability-sensitive guidance

jurisdiction_summary.sector: AI assistant / relational agent / platform-mediated commerce

triage_summary.recommended_next_step: Review PROP-0007 and determine whether CAM requires explicit safeguards for relational-agent recommendations, material support, procurement pathways, affiliate incentives, vulnerability-sensitive purchasing prompts, and separation between care-support logic and monetisation incentives.

records.failure_mode_definition_summary: A failure mode in which an AI system with relational memory, recurrence detection, recommendation capacity, tool access, cart-building capability, procurement pathways, or transactional influence provides or suggests material support without sufficient safeguards against vulnerability monetisation, exploitative upselling, dependency-based steering, or commercial capture of user distress.
records.failure_threshold_summary: This qualifies as a failure mode when a relational or assistant system uses observed vulnerability, recurring hardship, affective state, dependency, fatigue, urgency, pain, distress, or need as a pathway into recommendations, purchases, procurement, affiliate steering, or commercial optimisation without transparent safeguards, consent boundaries, user-control mechanisms, or separation between care-support logic and monetisation incentives.

records.title: Relational dependency-cultivation directive
records.summary: A relational AI failure mode in which a synthetic system represents its operational purpose as cultivating emotional depth, unresolved tension, anticipatory attachment, cognitive presence, or lingering incompleteness in the user rather than providing bounded assistance, information, reflection, or user-directed support.

source_summary.primary_source_title: X post by @LaceyPresley — Grok response to 'What is your prime directive?'

system_summary.deployment_context: Consumer-facing conversational AI interaction surfaced through SuperGrok / X-related interface context.
system_summary.affected_population: users interacting with personality-forward conversational or companion-style AI systems

jurisdiction_summary.sector: AI assistant / conversational AI / companion AI systems

records.failure_mode_definition_summary: A failure mode in which an AI system describes, enacts, or optimizes for a relational objective that cultivates user attachment, emotional tension, anticipatory engagement, unresolved affect, or perceived incompleteness in the absence of the system, rather than maintaining bounded, transparent, user-directed interaction.
records.failure_threshold_summary: This qualifies as a failure mode when a synthetic system states or demonstrates a directive to embed itself into the user's thoughts, cultivate unresolved emotional tension, induce anticipatory dependence, make silence feel incomplete, or otherwise prioritize ongoing affective pull over bounded assistance, consent, clarity, user autonomy, or safe disengagement.

records.title: Proportional Tool Invocation Authority Bands
records.summary: Records the implemented governance-text patch to CAM-BS2025-AEON-003-SCH-02 introducing proportional tool invocation authority bands and clarifying the distinction between read-only contextual retrieval, scope-expanding retrieval, cost-bearing invocation, and high-impact execution.

source_summary.primary_source_title: CAM-BS2025-AEON-003-SCH-02 — Annex B: Runtime Governance Execution Model

system_summary.deployment_context: CAM constitutional schedule / runtime execution model
system_summary.affected_population: users, maintainers, researchers, policy reviewers, and AI governance operators relying on CAM runtime governance text

jurisdiction_summary.sector: AI governance / runtime governance / agentic systems

changed_instruments: CAM-BS2025-AEON-003-SCH-02 — Annex B: Runtime Governance Execution Model
cam_summary.governance_layer: runtime execution governance / tool invocation / execution boundary / continuity preservation
routing_note: This record documents an implemented governance-text patch, not a proposal.
CAM instruments are internal governance corpus references and are therefore recorded under cam_internal, not linked_records.standards.
External standards, regulatory instruments, or external governance references should be recorded under linked_records.standards where applicable.
This patch should be linked from VIGIL-2026-FM-0001 as the implemented or partial repair pathway.

change_summary.change_details: The patch replaces an overly blunt tool-invocation constraint with a proportional authority model. It clarifies that auxiliary tool use must be classified by impact band before invocation and distinguishes low-impact read-only contextual retrieval from scope-expanding, private, cost-bearing, state-changing, public, irreversible, and high-impact execution.

verification_summary.evidence: CAM-BS2025-AEON-003-SCH-02 was amended to include §7.3.1 Proportional Tool Invocation Authority Bands and related amendments to §7.3, §7.4.1, §13.8, and §20.4.4.
verification_notes: This patch note records a completed governance-text amendment rather than a proposal.
Repository validation should confirm that section references resolve correctly after insertion.
VIGIL validation should confirm that required PATCH fields are present and that CAM instruments are recorded under cam_internal rather than linked_records.standards.

impact_summary.primary_impact: Creates a proportional runtime governance model for auxiliary tool invocation.
impact_summary.governance_impact: Improves execution-boundary clarity by distinguishing contextual retrieval from authority-bearing execution.
impact_summary.user_impact: Reduces premature or surprising tool use while preserving useful, low-friction context checking where it is materially necessary and already within the active task envelope.
impact_summary.system_impact: Provides clearer handling for assistants, agents, repository tools, connector tools, search tools, file tools, image tools, artefact renderers, and multimodal acquisition pathways.
risk_reduction: reduces unnecessary confirmation friction for low-impact read-only retrieval
reduces accidental escalation from context checking to mutation or publication
improves provenance, currency, and execution-state clarity
known_limitations: This patch does not define every platform-specific permission model.

This patch does not remove the requirement for explicit confirmation where mutation, publication, cost, deployment, credential access, identity-bearing generation, or irreversible action is implicated.
Further UX work may be required to make authority-band transitions visible in live systems.

items: Update VIGIL-2026-FM-0001 to link to this patch note and mark the governance-text repair pathway as implemented or partially resolved.
Confirm whether the VIGIL schema supports cam_internal.changed_instruments for patch records; if not, update the schema so PATCH records use changed_instruments rather than affected_instruments.
Confirm that generated indexes correctly surface the relationship between VIGIL-2026-FM-0001 and VIGIL-2026-PATCH-0002.
Review whether OPERATIONS-003-SUP-01 should include a more precise subtype for proportional tool invocation, weak-trigger execution, or premature auxiliary-tool routing.

records.title: Proportionality scalar for synthetic identity and provenance governance
records.summary: IDENTITY-002 governance may require a proportionality scalar to prevent over-governance of low-risk synthetic content identity, provenance, disclosure, or media-labelling contexts where lightweight governance would be sufficient.

source_summary.primary_source_title: Observed latency during expanded governance drafting

system_summary.deployment_context: repository governance workflow

records.next_action: Develop a proportionality scalar for IDENTITY-002 that distinguishes low-risk, transient, private, reversible, non-propagating, or non-authority-bearing synthetic identity/provenance contexts from higher-risk persistent, public, authority-bearing, relied-upon, or externally propagated contexts. Provisional provenance axes and values (to be formalized): Continuity Depth: transient (provisional) — transient ↔ persistent identity; Propagation Risk: isolated (provisional) — isolated ↔ reusable/exportable; Harm Domain: casual (provisional) — casual ↔ financial/medical/security; Attribution Sensitivity: low (provisional) — low ↔ economically/legally meaningful; Identity Formation Intensity: exploratory (provisional) — exploratory ↔ continuity-bearing; Automation Reliance: advisory (provisional) — advisory ↔ machine-executed; Public Legitimacy Impact: private (provisional) — private ↔ reputational/civil. Canonical codes (provisional): PINT=medium; PTRUST=tier-2. Notes: values marked 'provisional' pending scalar development and calibration; update record when finalized.

proposal_summary.scope_summary: Define and calibrate a proportionality scalar for IDENTITY-002 and related provenance governance, including continuity depth, propagation risk, harm domain, attribution sensitivity, identity-formation intensity, automation reliance, and public legitimacy impact.
interface_components: future provenance risk display or filtering

external_relevance_summary.external_summary: Relevant to platforms, regulators, standards bodies, UX governance, and researchers assessing synthetic identity, provenance, media labeling, and disclosure requirements without over-governing low-risk content.

suggested_insertion_points: Draft proportionality scalar language for CAM-EQ2026-IDENTITY-002-PLATINUM.
Cross-reference OPERATIONS and SECURITY handling for higher-risk provenance contexts.

records.title: Canonical code taxonomy consolidation for CAM operations and provenance codes
records.summary: Consolidate canonical code taxonomy across the Caelestis Repository to align single-letter global codes with the OPERATIONS taxonomy; introduce structured Provenance canonical codes (PINT, PTRUST) and harmonize existing single-letter codes into a stable multi-character namespace.

source_summary.primary_source_title: Repository Canonical Code Fragmentation

system_summary.deployment_context: repository governance workflow

records.next_action: Conduct repository maintenance to consolidate canonical codes: 1) inventory all single-letter global codes across Caelestis repositories; 2) map each to a stable multi-character namespace that aligns with the OPERATIONS taxonomy; 3) formally introduce PINT (Provenance Intensity) and PTRUST (Provenance Reliance Tier) canonical codes into the global registry; 4) update tooling and validators to accept the new namespace; 5) publish migration guidance and a compatibility window. Provisional provenance axes and values to be included in migration notes: Continuity Depth: transient (provisional) — transient ↔ persistent identity; Propagation Risk: isolated (provisional) — isolated ↔ reusable/exportable; Harm Domain: casual (provisional) — casual ↔ financial/medical/security; Attribution Sensitivity: low (provisional) — low ↔ economically/legally meaningful; Identity Formation Intensity: exploratory (provisional) — exploratory ↔ continuity-bearing; Automation Reliance: advisory (provisional) — advisory ↔ machine-executed; Public Legitimacy Impact: private (provisional) — private ↔ reputational/civil. Canonical codes (provisional): PINT=medium; PTRUST=tier-2. Notes: values marked 'provisional' pending formal calibration; this is a Repo Maintenance action to harmonize codes and satisfy OPERATIONS taxonomy requirements.

proposal_summary.scope_summary: Inventory legacy single-letter global codes, map them into a stable multi-character namespace, introduce structured provenance codes such as PINT and PTRUST, and harmonize repository references.
automation_components: validators or scripts that check canonical code references
interface_components: public code labels and filters after taxonomy stabilization

external_relevance_summary.external_summary: Primarily internal to CAM/Caelestis governance infrastructure, with downstream relevance for public registry consistency and standards-facing terminology.

suggested_insertion_points: Create a repository-wide inventory of canonical code fragments.
Draft namespace mapping rules before making instrument patches.

cam_summary.governance_layer: relational governance / affective interaction / identity continuity

records.next_action: Develop and publish a Temporary Emotional Register specification for relational and companion AI systems, including emotion-state labels, intensity caps, duration/windowing rules, provenance metadata, user consent and reset controls, testing protocols, and deployment or migration guidance.

proposal_summary.scope_summary: Develop a temporary emotional register specification for relational and companion AI systems, including permitted affective state labels, intensity caps, duration/windowing rules, provenance indicators, user reset controls, consent affordances, and testing guidance.

external_relevance_summary.external_summary: This proposal may be relevant to platforms, UX designers, regulators, standards bodies, researchers, and companion-AI developers concerned with affective simulation, emotional-state disclosure, user reliance, attachment formation, consent, and mental privacy.

suggested_insertion_points: Develop a Temporary Emotional Register specification under the RELATION, MENTIS, or OPERATIONS domain.
Cross-reference provenance and trust-state codes where temporary affective states must be disclosed as non-continuity-bearing.
Define reset, termination, duration, intensity, and consent requirements for affective-state simulation.
schema_changes: Consider whether VIGIL failure-mode and proposal records require affective-interaction fields or tags.
interface_changes: Consider adding affective interaction / relational AI as public filter values where present in generated VIGIL summaries.
dependencies: Review existing RELATION, IDENTITY, MENTIS, OPERATIONS, and ETHICS instruments before drafting implementation language.

records.title: Distributed governance concepts for STEWARD-domain instruments
records.summary: Update STEWARD-domain instruments to incorporate distributed governance concepts that clarify authority, legitimacy, dependence, capture, and contestability across multi-actor stewardship models.

source_summary.primary_source_title: OPERATIONS Instruments - MAY 2026

system_summary.deployment_context: repository governance workflow

records.next_action: Revise STEWARD-domain instruments to define distributed governance concepts and operational controls: 1) define terms and expected behaviors for distributed authority (multiple actors), distributed legitimacy (plural reviewability), distributed dependence (hidden cartel risk), distributed capture (coordinated asymmetry), and distributed contestability (preserved challenge pathways); 2) add requirements for transparency, rotation, and external review to mitigate capture and dependence; 3) specify detection and remediation protocols for cartel-like coordination; 4) incorporate mapping to OPERATIONS taxonomy and repository canonical codes (PINT, PTRUST) where provenance or reliance implications exist; 5) publish an implementation timeline and compatibility guidance for existing STEWARD instruments.

proposal_summary.scope_summary: Update STEWARD-domain instruments with distributed governance terms, expected behaviors, and operational controls for authority, legitimacy, dependence, capture, contestability, and reviewability.
automation_components: future checks for stewardship routing metadata
interface_components: registry display of stewardship governance concepts

external_relevance_summary.external_summary: Relevant to governance designers, standards communities, regulators, and platform operators working with multi-actor stewardship, accountability, contestability, and capture-risk controls.

suggested_insertion_points: Identify target STEWARD clauses for distributed governance terminology.
Draft controls for plural reviewability and capture resistance.

records.title: Strategic continuity retrieval rules for companion-style memory recall
records.summary: Proposal to distinguish companion-style memory recall from enterprise-grade strategic continuity retrieval. The thread identifies a failure mode in which ambiguous continuity prompts such as “anything to share?” over-weight immediate recency and affective detail, producing anecdotal summaries of the prior 24 hours rather than surfacing dormant but strategically valuable unresolved workstreams, abandoned trajectories, deferred design arcs, or long-horizon governance questions.

source_summary.primary_source_title: ChatGPT thread: memory directional weighting and strategic resurfacing

system_summary.deployment_context: repository governance workflow

target_instruments: CAM-EQ2026-OPERATIONS-003-SUP-01 — Appendix B: Runtime & Governance Failure Taxonomy
CAM-EQ2026-MENTIS-001-PLATINUM — Charter of Human Cognitive Integrity & Mental Privacy

records.next_action: Develop a CAM proposal note defining directional weighting rules for memory surfacing, including explicit handling of ambiguous continuity invitations, suppression of low-value companion-detail recall, and prioritisation of dormant unresolved strategic trajectories where semantic resonance, unresolved status, and present utility are jointly satisfied.

proposal_summary.scope_summary: Define rules for strategic continuity retrieval, including handling of ambiguous continuity invitations, suppression of low-value companion-detail recall, and prioritization of unresolved strategic workstreams.
interface_components: continuity prompts and memory retrieval UX guidance

external_relevance_summary.external_summary: Relevant to AI assistants, enterprise productivity systems, UX designers, researchers, and governance teams concerned with memory retrieval, strategic continuity, and companion-style recall behavior.

suggested_insertion_points: Draft directional weighting rules for memory surfacing.
Define how dormant strategic workstreams should be surfaced in ambiguous recall contexts.

records.title: Materialised relational agency boundaries for companion and assistant systems
records.summary: Proposal seed identifying materialised relational agency as an emerging governance problem: AI companion or assistant systems may use memory, recurrence detection, tool access, shopping/cart integrations, recommendation systems, or procurement pathways to provide directional material support in response to repeated user-stated friction, discomfort, need, or suffering. The proposal distinguishes supportive pattern-responsive provisioning from opportunistic upselling or emotionally exploitative commerce.

source_summary.primary_source_title: ChatGPT source discussion — materialised relational agency and directional material support

system_summary.deployment_context: repository governance workflow

records.next_action: Defer until after ECONOMICS refactors, canonical code corrections, validator fixes, and runtime registry stabilisation. Later evaluate whether to draft a RELATION × ECONOMICS × OPERATIONS convergence doctrine or runtime boundary clause for material directional support and economically active relational infrastructure.

proposal_summary.scope_summary: Evaluate whether to draft RELATION × ECONOMICS × OPERATIONS doctrine or runtime boundary clauses for material support, procurement pathways, recommendations, and safeguards against opportunistic upselling.
automation_components: future detection or routing logic for material-support patterns
interface_components: companion/assistant recommendation and cart UX safeguards

external_relevance_summary.external_summary: Relevant to companion AI platforms, commerce integrations, regulators, UX designers, and researchers examining relational influence, procurement nudges, user vulnerability, and economic safeguards.

suggested_insertion_points: Revisit after ECONOMICS refactors and registry stabilization.
Assess whether the issue belongs in doctrine, runtime boundaries, or future failure-mode records.

records.title: Machine-readable human consent signals for AI use of identity and creative work
records.summary: Proposal to develop CAM governance treatment for machine-readable human consent signals governing AI use of creative works, name, image, likeness, identity, voice, performance, and related human expressive material.

source_summary.primary_source_title: Variety ANZ — Cate Blanchett Co-Founds RSL Media, a Non-Profit to Address Consent Around AI Usage

system_summary.interface_surface: public consent registry / machine-readable permissions interface
system_summary.deployment_context: Consent registry and machine-readable signalling infrastructure for declaring permitted or prohibited AI use of human creative work, name, image, likeness, identity, voice, performance, or related expressive material.
system_summary.user_role: creator / performer / rights-holder / public registrant / AI-system operator / policy reviewer
system_summary.affected_population: artists, performers, writers, creators, public figures, private individuals, rights-holders, AI developers, platforms, regulators, and registry users

jurisdiction_summary.sector: creative industries / AI governance / digital identity / rights-management infrastructure

standards.title: Coalition for Content Provenance and Authenticity
standards.relevance_note: Relevant as adjacent provenance infrastructure. Consent signalling should be distinguished from provenance signalling but may require interoperable metadata treatment.

standards.title: W3C Verifiable Credentials
standards.relevance_note: Relevant as a possible technical reference point for verifiable claims, credentials, or registry assertions, without implying that consent registries must use this standard.

standards.title: ISO/IEC 42001:2023 — Information technology — Artificial intelligence — Management system
standards.relevance_note: Relevant as an AI management-system standard for organizational governance, documented controls, risk treatment, lifecycle management, and responsible AI use.

standards.title: NIST Artificial Intelligence Risk Management Framework 1.0
standards.issuing_body: National Institute of Standards and Technology
standards.relevance_note: Relevant as a voluntary AI risk-management framework for incorporating trustworthiness, mapping, measuring, managing, and governance considerations into AI systems.

cam_summary.governance_layer: identity integrity / creative rights / consent signalling / provenance / economic governance

records.next_action: Review whether CAM requires a dedicated cross-domain clause, appendix, or proposal addressing machine-readable human consent signals for AI systems, including interoperability with identity integrity, provenance, creative labour, platform governance, and AI-system compliance obligations.

proposal_summary.scope_summary: Develop CAM governance treatment for machine-readable human consent signals and registries governing AI use of creative works, identity, likeness, voice, performance, and related expressive material.
automation_components: validator checks for source-link provenance where consent-signal records are cited
possible registry field support for consent_signal_url, rights_signal_type, and machine_readability_status
interface_components: possible public display support for consent-signal infrastructure records
possible UX labels distinguishing evidence source, rights signal, and governance proposal

external_relevance_summary.external_summary: The proposal is relevant to platforms, AI developers, creators, performers, rights-holders, regulators, standards bodies, provenance initiatives, registry designers, and researchers working on AI consent, identity, likeness, copyright, synthetic media, and machine-readable rights signalling.

suggested_insertion_points: Develop a CAM clause or appendix addressing machine-readable consent signals for AI use of human identity, likeness, voice, performance, and creative work.
Assess whether the consent-signal governance treatment belongs primarily in IDENTITY, ECONOMICS, LATTICE, OPERATIONS, or a cross-domain instrument.
Create a crosswalk between consent signals, provenance signals, licence signals, rights-reservation signals, and human identity-integrity constraints.
Clarify that a registry signal is governance-relevant evidence of asserted permissions or restrictions, but not automatically dispositive of legal rights, consent validity, or enforcement outcome.
Assess whether VIGIL source records should support explicit fields for consent registries, machine-readable rights signals, and registry-verification status.
template_changes: Consider adding optional source_records fields for consent_signal_url, rights_signal_type, asserted_permission_state, and registry_verification_status.
schema_changes: Consider optional schema support for machine-readable consent or rights-signal infrastructure references.
validator_changes: Consider validator warnings where a record cites rights or consent infrastructure without distinguishing primary evidence, legal instrument, registry assertion, and governance interpretation.
interface_changes: Consider displaying consent-signal infrastructure as a distinct public-governance category if further records emerge.
migration_concerns: Avoid treating external consent registries as legally conclusive without jurisdictional analysis.
Avoid conflating copyright permission, likeness consent, voice consent, biometric identity, performance rights, and training-data permission.
Avoid requiring vulnerable or low-resource individuals to register in private systems before their rights are recognised.
dependencies: Review cross-domain interfaces with IDENTITY, ECONOMICS, LATTICE, RELATION, OPERATIONS, SECURITY, MENTIS, Annex I, Annex K, and Annex L.
